rationale: Authority data for W40K-0101..W40K-0110 — the 2019-2020 cluster spanning the 9th-edition lead-up: Adepta Sororitas as primary protagonist faction (twice: *Mark of Faith* + *Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint*, plus secondary in *Requiem Infernal*), Necrons as primary protagonist faction (twice: *The Infinite and the Divine* + co-primary in *Indomitus*), Orks as primary protagonist faction (*Brutal Kunnin*, first authority-layer Ork-POV), Navis Nobilite as primary protagonist faction (*Rites of Passage*, first authority-layer Navigator-POV novel), Carcharodons / Space Sharks as primary protagonist faction (*Silent Hunters*, first authority-layer Carcharodons entry). Bookends: Rachel Harrison's *Honourbound* (2019, Severina Raine commissar-POV opener — Eleventh Antari Rifles in the Bale Stars Crusade vs the Sighted Chaos cult, three-arc structure with present campaign + Schola-Progenium/sister-Lucia flashbacks + intra-Crusade malfeasance subplot) and Edoardo Albert's *Silent Hunters* (2021, Carcharodons brothers-separated-by-feud novel — Pale Nomad Te Kahurangi + Chaplain Tangata Manu pursuing a stolen relic into Commorragh / the Dark City; Chapter-serf POVs Iraia + Jona). Heart of the batch: Peter Fehervari's *Requiem Infernal* (2019, Sister-Hospitaller Asenath Hyades returns to her Last-Candle birthworld Candleworld escorting traumatised Astra-Militarum survivors of a xenos encounter — Fehervari Dark-Coil register, malign forces stirring in storm-wracked spires + insidious shadows in the seekers' souls; Jonah Tythe parallel-pilgrimage POV), Guy Haley's *Belisarius Cawl: The Great Work* (2019, Cawl + Tetrarch Felix + Scythes-of-the-Emperor remnants on the ruined homeworld Sotha — Necron tomb-raid under the chapter monastery + Cawl reckoning with 10,000 years of lost/deleted/stolen memories, ancient evil + residual Tyranid infestation), Mike Brooks's *Rites of Passage* (2019, Lady Chettamandey Brobantis Navigator-POV — frail-old-woman-with-sharp-tongue widow-and-murderer of Lord Azariel vs Chaos cultist Radimir Niklau harvesting Navigator warp-eyes after his Galimo-Prime ritual on Vorlese; first authority-layer entry with House Brobantis + Navis Nobilite as primary protagonist), Rachel Harrison's *Mark of Faith* (2020, Sister Evangeline of Order of Our Martyred Lady + Inquisitor Ahri Ravara crossing the Great Rift into Imperium Nihilus to recover the Shield of Saint Katherine — Ravara's Thousand-Sons-Sorcerer subplot with the crippled-interrogator-Sofika in a miniature Golden Throne), David Annandale's *Ephrael Stern: The Heretic Saint* (2020, Stern the Thrice-Born / Daemonifuge / Heretic Saint + Harlequin Kyganil 100-year webway pilgrimage to gather holy relics and reach the Black Library — webway encounter with Yvraine, Herald of Ynnead, frames the Imperium-Nihilus / Guilliman-returns context), Mike Brooks's *Brutal Kunnin* (2020, Goff Big-Mek Ufthak Blackhawk vs Adeptus-Mechanicus forge world Hephaesto + Freebooterz Kaptin Badrukk + grot Nizkwik + Mogrot Redtoof — first authority-layer entry where Orks are the primary protagonist faction, dropping grimdark for `satirical + action_heavy` register), Robert Rath's *The Infinite and the Divine* (2020, dual-Necron rivalry between Trazyn the Infinite and Orikan the Diviner — Orikan needs a device from Trazyn's Solemnace galleries to find the last true Necrontyr Phaeron's tomb on Cepharil/Serenade; Triarch Council forces them into co-op against a Genestealer-Cult uprising and the resulting Imperial exterminatus; spans millennia, ends with Trazyn-escape + Orikan-stranded), and Gav Thorpe's *Indomitus* (2020, 9th-edition launch novel — Crusade Fleet Quintus's *Ithraca's Vengeance* under Captain Aeschelus + Lts Nemetus and Praxamedes stranded in a becalmed-warp system inside the Pariah Nexus; dual-POV with Necron Overlord Simut + Plasmancer Ah-hotep + Skorpekh-Lord Zozar; the choice between last-stand against the Silent Kingdom and break-away-to-warn-Guilliman is the structural pivot). Structural notes: a heavy female-POV batch — Severina Raine + Asenath Hyades + Lady Chettamandey + Evangeline / Ravara + Ephrael Stern — **five of ten books have female primary POVs**, the strongest such ratio in the authority layer to date. Dense new first-authority-layer surface-forms: Eleventh Antari Rifles + the Sighted Cult + Bale Stars + Order of the Last Candle/Eternal Candle + Candleworld + House Brobantis + Vorlese + Galimo Prime + Lord Azariel + Lady Chettamandey Brobantis + Radimir Niklau + Sotha + Scythes of the Emperor + Tetrarch Felix + Order of Our Martyred Lady + Ophelia VII + Shield of Saint Katherine + Inquisitor Ahri Ravara + Sister Evangeline + Sister Augusta + Sofika + Ephrael Stern (Thrice-Born / Daemonifuge / Heretic Saint) + Kyganil + Yvraine + Ynnead / Ynnari + Hephaesto + Ufthak Blackhawk + Mogrot Redtoof + Nizkwik + Kaptin Badrukk + Da Biggest Big Mek + Cepharil / Serenade + Solemnace + Trazyn the Infinite + Orikan the Diviner + Triarch Council + Crusade Fleet Quintus + Ithraca's Vengeance + Captain Aeschelus + Lt Nemetus + Lt Praxamedes + Overlord Simut + Plasmancer Ah-hotep + Skorpekh-Lord Zozar + Pariah Nexus + Silent Kingdom + Carcharodons / Space Sharks + Te Kahurangi (Pale Nomad) + Chaplain Tangata Manu + Iraia + Jona + Sagaraya + Commorragh / Dark City. No `data_conflict` flags this iteration — all ten roster entries are correctly typed as `novel` per Black-Library / Lexicanum / Track-of-Words. No `low_confidence` flags either — all ten entries have strong post-2019 Black-Library coverage. **Resolver-Pre-Note:** cumulativeBefore=100 (Brief-061 algorithm's strict reading: 100 % 50 == 0 → resolver-pause due). Maintainer invoked `skip-50-stop` marker in the session-opening prompt, so the pre-check was overridden; the resolver-pause-block is deferred. Status-log entry annotates `skip-50-stop active` explicitly.

overrides.synopsis: Standalone series-opener (Severina Raine arc, book 1 of the Bale-Stars Crusade thread). Commissar Severina Raine — Schola-Progenium-trained, Antari by adoption — serves with the Eleventh Antari Rifles in the **Bale Stars Crusade**, the Astra-Militarum push to extinguish the **Sighted**, a Chaos cult fielding bound-thrall regiments across the Bale-Stars constellation. The novel runs three converging arcs: present-day campaign across three large set-pieces — drop-strike, urban grind, and final cult-citadel assault — as the Antari hunt the Sighted's command structure; flashbacks to Raine's youth at the Schola Progenium with her sister Lucia, tracing how a Commissar is made and what loyalty to the Antari cost her; and a slow-burning intra-Crusade malfeasance subplot in which someone inside the Crusade's upper echelons is letting the Sighted know where the hammer is about to fall. Tone is somber-grimdark, character-study spine, war-story register. **First authority-layer entry with a Commissar as primary POV** — protagonist_class tagged `guardsman` per the Cain/Gaunt workaround until a `commissar` value is promoted; also the **first authority-layer entry where the Antari Rifles + the Sighted + the Bale Stars Crusade appear as surface forms**.

Standalone Adepta-Sororitas-Hospitaller novel in Peter Fehervari's loose **Dark Coil** continuity. The **Order of the Last Candle** — a heterodox Sororitas convent encompassing Militant, Hospitaller and Dialogus branches — has stood vigil for centuries on its storm-wracked sanctuary world, **Candleworld**, deciphering the tormented visions of the order's founder and keeping outsiders away. Sister-Hospitaller **Asenath Hyades**, a daughter of that world who turned her back on the order decades ago, returns leading the decimated survivors of an elite Astra-Militarum company — broken by an encounter with a lethal xenos entity and seeking healing the Imperium proper cannot give. Asenath's stated mission is to investigate rumours that Candleworld's custodians have fallen to heresy; her private one is to lay her own ghosts to rest. Travelling with her, the itinerant preacher **Jonah Tythe** carries his own pilgrimage and his own past lives. As the seekers near the convent's bastion, malign forces stir among the spires and the most insidious shadows turn out to lie in the seekers' own souls. Cosmic-horror + mystery + character-study register; faith-and-doubt as plot engine rather than backdrop. Inquisition-consistency: not triggered (Hospitaller-POV, no Inquisitor on-page).

Standalone Archmagos-Dominus novel set in the wake of the Great Rift. **Belisarius Cawl** — Mars's most heretical genius, the 10,000-year-old architect of the Primaris project — turns the next phase of his secret **Great Work** to the abandoned world of **Sotha**, once the homeworld of the **Scythes of the Emperor** before Hive Fleet Kraken broke them. Accompanied by **Tetrarch Felix** and his elite Ultramarines warriors, plus what remains of the Scythes themselves, Cawl ventures into the Necron tomb-network buried beneath the chapter's old monastery, ostensibly to purge the last of the residual Tyranid infestation but in truth to recover an artefact he believes is the lynchpin of the Great Work. Tomb-raiding on a scale no other Tech-Priest has ever attempted; an ancient Necron evil that has been waiting under Sotha since before the chapter's founding; and a parallel inward journey in which Cawl is forced to confront the 10,000 years of memories he has lost, deleted, or had stolen from himself. Heist + mystery + war-story register; philosophical tone over the standard grimdark. **First authority-layer entry where an Adeptus-Mechanicus Archmagos is the primary POV and where Sotha + Scythes of the Emperor + Tetrarch Felix appear as surface forms.**

Standalone Navis-Nobilite novel (Lady Chettamandey series, book 1). **Lord Azariel**, head of the Navigator House Brobantis, is dead — quietly murdered by his frail elderly widow, **Lady Chettamandey Brobantis**, whose sharp tongue and sharper intellect have positioned her to inherit House Brobantis's power and influence in full. Her plans for ascension are derailed when she's drawn into a dark spiral of ritual killings and cult murders aimed at Navigators specifically: planets are being dragged into the warp seemingly at a whim, and the third eyes of Navis-Nobilite victims — the warp-eyes through which Navigators see the warp and guide Imperial transit — are being harvested by a single, very dangerous hand. That hand belongs to **Radimir Niklau**, fresh from a world-ending ritual on **Galimo Prime** and now installed on **Vorlese**, ingratiated with a local Chaos cult, using his warp powers for surgical assassinations. The novel is a court-intrigue-and-mystery procedural inside the Navigator Houses, with Chettamandey's frail-old-woman register set against Niklau's ritual-murderer register. **First authority-layer entry where the Navis Nobilite + House Brobantis are the primary protagonist faction**; protagonist_class tagged `civilian` (the closest available value for a Navigator).

Standalone Adepta-Sororitas novel set after the war for **Ophelia VII**. Battle-Sister **Evangeline** of the **Order of Our Martyred Lady** is tasked with a crusade-pilgrimage across the **Great Rift** into **Imperium Nihilus** to recover the **Shield of Saint Katherine**, one of the relics torn loose from the Imperium when the Cicatrix Maledictum opened. She travels with the enigmatic **Inquisitor Ahri Ravara** of the Ordo Hereticus, whose own reasons for the crossing are not the Order's. The novel alternates Evangeline's POV — faith newly raw after Ophelia VII, working out what miracle and martyrdom mean when the Emperor's light is intermittent — with Ravara's POV, dragging her own dark past behind her: a brutal encounter with a Thousand-Sons sorcerer left her interrogator and lover **Sofika** crippled and interred in a miniature mockery of the Golden Throne, and the trail of that sorcerer reaches into the Nihilus territory the pilgrimage now crosses. **Sister Augusta** travels with them as Evangeline's anchor and shield. War-story + journey + political-thriller register; faith and doubt as twin spines. Inquisition-consistency: Ravara is Ordo Hereticus, Inquisition faction tagged with role=primary.

Standalone Adepta-Sororitas-mythic-arc novel — the prose follow-up to the *Daemonifuge* graphic-novel mythology. **Ephrael Stern**, known across the tortured galaxy as the **Thrice-Born**, the **Daemonifuge**, and the **Heretic Saint**, finds herself forsaken when the Great Rift dawns and the Emperor's holy light is extinguished from her. For roughly a hundred years she has travelled the Imperium with her **Harlequin companion Kyganil**, jumping via the webway from one daemon-haunted world to the next, purging daemons and gathering holy relics. Bereft, she is found by a mysterious stranger who offers her a new destiny — one that might yet see the Imperium saved. The novel follows Stern and Kyganil as they slip back into the webway and meet **Yvraine, Herald of Ynnead**, who updates Stern on the return of Roboute Guilliman and the Indomitus Crusade into **Imperium Nihilus**; their search arcs toward the **Black Library** as the seat of the knowledge Stern needs. Cosmic-horror + journey + character-study register; protagonist is Sororitas-by-origin but operates outside Imperial sanction, hence the title. **First authority-layer entry with Harlequins + the Ynnari + Yvraine as on-page characters and with the Black Library as a sought destination.**

Standalone Ork-POV novel — the **first authority-layer entry where Orks are the primary protagonist faction**. **Ufthak Blackhawk**, a Goff Big-Mek with a counter-intuitive kind of brains, leads the green tide down onto the **Adeptus-Mechanicus forge world Hephaesto** only to discover the planet is already under siege by the notorious Freebooterz **Kaptin Badrukk** and his crew. **Da Biggest Big Mek** (Ufthak's warboss) orders temporary co-operation between the two Ork armies, and Ufthak — sensing a name-making chance — sets about crushing the Imperium's most advanced defenders and claiming the greatest prize on the world. Standing in his way: a sinister new war-machine on the Imperial side, Badrukk's parallel plotting and double-crossing, and a thoroughly annoying grot named **Nizkwik** who is simultaneously eager to please and terrified. POV is mostly Ufthak and his minder-grot, with periodic Adeptus-Mechanicus / Skitarii chapters showing the forge-world defence. **Tone is satirical + action_heavy, NOT grimdark** — the Brooks Ork-prose register is comedy-grim, similar to the Cain-novels exception but inverted. Surface-forms: Ufthak Blackhawk, Mogrot Redtoof, Nizkwik, Kaptin Badrukk, Hephaesto, Da Biggest Big Mek.

Standalone dual-Necron novel — the **first authority-layer entry where Necrons are the sole primary protagonist faction**. **Trazyn the Infinite**, Overlord of **Solemnace** and obsessive collector of the galaxy's artefacts, lives behind the wards of his impossible galleries. **Orikan the Diviner**, chronomancer-prophet of the Sautekh dynasty, breaks into those galleries to seize a single esoteric device that will let him locate the long-lost tomb of the **last true Necrontyr Phaeron** — and, through it, the knowledge to reverse biotransference and return the Necrons to living bodies. The theft ignites a rivalry that spans millennia. Forced by the **Triarch Council** into uneasy co-operation, Trazyn and Orikan locate the tomb on **Cepharil** (formerly the tomb-world Cephris, locally renamed **Serenade** by humans drawn down by a mysterious signal from beneath the surface). A Genestealer-Cult uprising on Cepharil pushes the Imperium toward planetary bombardment; the two Necrons race the exterminatus to the Eternity Gate. Trazyn escapes; Orikan does not. Philosophical + action-heavy + satirical register (the dual-Necron prose has a quiet comedy of antiquated arrogance). Surface-forms: Trazyn the Infinite, Orikan the Diviner, Solemnace, Cepharil/Serenade/Cephris, Triarch Council, the Eternity Gate, the last true Necrontyr Phaeron.

Standalone Indomitus-Crusade frame novel — the 9th-edition launch tie-in. The **Crusade Fleet Quintus** Ultramarines-cruiser **Ithraca's Vengeance**, in desperate need of a comprehensive victory after a string of inconclusive engagements, finds the warp around it become strangely becalmed and the ship stranded inside a system afflicted by a bizarre psychic malaise. The malaise has a name: this is the heart of the **Pariah Nexus**, the Necron technology-grid by which the **Silent Kingdom** harvests souls from millions of enslaved subjects to power its expansion. Three Ultramarines POVs alternate — the relatively inexperienced **Captain Aeschelus**, the action-oriented **Lieutenant Nemetus**, and the reserved, more experienced **Lieutenant Praxamedes** — against three Necron POVs: the paranoiac **Overlord Simut**, the somewhat insubordinate **Plasmancer Ah-hotep**, and the destruction-obsessed **Skorpekh Lord Zozar**. The structural pivot is a forced choice: mount a desperate last stand to destroy the Pariah Nexus and condemn the Ultramarines, or break away and damn the entire sector to bring word of this ancient foe's resurgence to **Lord Primarch Roboute Guilliman**. Last-stand + war-story + mystery register; explicit on-ramp for new readers entering the setting at the 9th-edition boundary.

Standalone Carcharodons-Astra chapter novel. The **Carcharodons** — the void-haunted Space-Sharks chapter that operates out of the Galactic fringe and almost never returns to Imperial space — are drawn back inward by the theft of a relic that **Chaplain Tangata Manu** has hunted for a thousand years. The hunt has reached its end just as the relic is taken from him, and the trail leads into the worst possible place: **Commorragh**, the **Dark City** of the **Drukhari**, where the chapter's prey is now hiding what belongs to the Space-Sharks. The novel pulls **Te Kahurangi**, the **Pale Nomad** Librarian familiar from Robbie MacNiven's earlier Carcharodons books, back onto the page — and reveals that he and Tangata Manu are brothers, separated by an ancient feud and a present rivalry that the relic-hunt forces into reconciliation. Two **chapter serfs**, **Iraia** and her son **Jona**, are drawn into the quest and supply the human-scale POV inside the Carcharodons' alien register. Settings range from the **Void** of the Galactic fringe to the sea world **Sagaraya** to Commorragh itself. **First authority-layer entry where the Carcharodons / Space Sharks chapter is the primary protagonist faction; first on-page Commorragh setting in the authority layer.** Journey + war-story + heist register; brotherhood and ancient-feud reconciliation as theme spine.
